---
title: "Imago"
subtitle: "A complete tour of the theme"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
lang: en
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: ../assets/imago.scss
    navigation-mode: linear
    controls-layout: bottom-right
    center: false
    slide-number: true
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-color: "#E2E5F3"
bibliography: ../assets/references.bib
---

# The palette

## Nine named colours

Imago ships a fixed palette. Every colour is available three ways: as a
**text** colour, a **background** colour, and (for three of them) a
**highlighter**.

[navy]{.bg-navy .white} ·
[blue]{.bg-blue .white} ·
[green]{.bg-green .white} ·
[orange]{.bg-orange .white} ·
[grey]{.bg-grey .imago-grey} ·
[light-grey]{.bg-light-grey .imago-grey} ·
[dark-grey]{.bg-dark-grey .white} ·
[imago-grey]{.bg-imago-grey .white}

| Token | Hex | Token | Hex |
|---|---|---|---|
| navy | `#24226F` | blue | `#1877CF` |
| green | `#03CEA3` | orange | `#FF8F42` |
| grey | `#B8BCD1` | light-grey | `#E2E5F3` |
| dark-grey | `#8C91A8` | imago-grey | `#4a4a49` |

## Text colours

Apply a colour to a run of words with a bracketed span: `[text]{.navy}`.

This sentence mixes [navy]{.navy}, [blue]{.blue}, [green]{.green},
[orange]{.orange}, [dark-grey]{.dark-grey} and [imago-grey]{.imago-grey}.

You can combine a colour with emphasis: **[bold orange]{.orange}**,
_[italic green]{.green}_.

::: notes
The full set of text classes: `.navy .blue .green .orange .grey
.light-grey .dark-grey .imago-grey .white`.
:::

## Highlighters

Three padded, rounded highlight boxes — `[…]{.hlg}` and friends:

The headline result was [up 42%]{.hlg} this quarter, with
[strong retention]{.hlg-green} and [no churn]{.hlg-blue} in the cohort.

- `.hlg` — orange (the default)
- `.hlg-green` — green
- `.hlg-blue` — blue

## Background blocks

The `.bg-*` classes shine on whole blocks. Put them on a fenced div and
pair with a text colour for contrast:

::: {.bg-navy .white}
**Navy block.** White text on navy — good for a callout or a key takeaway.
:::

::: {.bg-light-grey}
**Light-grey block.** Subtle panel for asides; text stays imago-grey.
:::

# Light & dark slides

## This is a light slide {.light}

Light is the default, but `{.light}` makes it explicit. Headings are navy,
body text is imago-grey, and [links are orange](https://quarto.org).

Add the class to the slide's `##` heading: `## Title {.light}`.

## This is a dark slide {.dark}

`{.dark}` flips the whole slide: imago-grey background, white text, white
headings, and [links turn green](https://quarto.org).

- Bullets stay readable
- [Coloured spans]{.orange} still work — [like this]{.green}
- Highlighters too: [emphasis]{.hlg}

# Layout

## Vertical centring is the default

Imago vertically centres slide content by default, so short slides sit in
the middle of the frame instead of clinging to the top.

This is great for chapter breaks and single-idea slides.

## Top-aligned content {.align-top}

Add `{.align-top}` to pin content to the top instead — useful when a slide
has a lot of material and you want it to read top-down.

1. First point
2. Second point
3. Third point

## Two columns with a caption

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="55%"}
::: {.bg-light-grey}
&nbsp;

**Figure area**

&nbsp;
:::
<small>A figure caption set in the `.column small` style — centred, muted dark-grey, with [orange links](https://quarto.org).</small>
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
**Captions** are styled automatically inside a `.column`: any `<small>` (or
a pandoc image caption) becomes centred, small, and dark-grey.

Drop a real image in with:

```markdown
![My caption](assets/photo.jpg)
```
:::

::::

# Typography & content

## Figtree, self-hosted

The theme is set in **Figtree** — a variable sans-serif bundled with the
project (no network needed). It carries the full weight range, so
**bold**, regular, and _italic_ all render from one font.

> “Typography is the craft of endowing human language with a durable
> visual form.” — a blockquote, styled by the theme.

## Lists & fragments

Reveal items one at a time by wrapping a list in `.incremental`:

::: {.incremental}
- Point one — visible immediately
- Point two — press **→**
- Point three — and so on
:::

## Code

Syntax highlighting, no execution:

```python
def greet(name: str) -> str:
    return f"Hello, {name}!"
```

Inline `code` is styled too.

## Tables

| Feature | Class | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Text colour | `.orange` | [orange]{.orange} |
| Highlight | `.hlg-green` | [green]{.hlg-green} |
| Background | `.bg-navy` | navy block |
| Dark slide | `{.dark}` | this row's theme |

## Math

Rendered by KaTeX, fully offline.

Inline: $E = mc^2$

Display:

$$
\hat{\boldsymbol{\beta}} = \bigl(\mathbf{X}^\top \mathbf{X}\bigr)^{-1}\mathbf{X}^\top \mathbf{y}
$$

## Compact text {.align-top}

Wrap dense material in `.small-font` to tighten line-height:

::: {.small-font}
This block uses the `.small-font` utility. It keeps the type at the base
size but tightens the leading, which helps when a slide carries a longer
passage of explanatory prose that would otherwise feel airy and spread out
across the frame.
:::

# Citations & references

## Citing sources

Declare a `.bib` in the frontmatter and cite inline. Slipway built on
Pandoc [@pandoc] and reveal.js [@revealjs]; the bibliography below is
generated into the themed `#refs` block.

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
